source_issue.title: Include diazo documentation in Classic backend documentation

summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue seeks to include the Diazo documentation into the Plone documentation repository. Currently, the Diazo documentation is hosted separately at [diazo.org](https://docs.diazo.org/en/latest/), and there is a suggestion to integrate it more closely with Plone’s documentation.

## What needs to be done
1. Find and contact the maintainers of the Diazo documentation on ReadTheDocs to request maintainer access.
2. Convert the existing Diazo documentation to MyST format, which is a format compatible with markdown.
3. Decide where to add these documents in the Plone repository as a Git submodule.
4. Set up the submodule in the `plone/documentation` following [these guidelines](https://6.docs.plone.org/contributing/documentation/admins.html#importing-external-docs-with-submodules).

## Helpful background
Diazo is a tool for theming in Plone, and integrating its documentation could enhance the user experience. The discussions are ongoing, and there might be additional criteria that need clarification.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with the Diazo documentation at [diazo.org](https://docs.diazo.org/en/latest/).
2. Check the [Plone documentation contribution guide](https://6.docs.plone.org/contributing/documentation/) for setup instructions.
3. Consider reaching out to the current maintainers for guidance as you begin this work.

Feel free to ask questions if you're unsure about anything!
```
